tasks.title: Add govulncheck job to CI workflow
tasks.description: Add govulncheck job to CI workflow
tasks.body: Add a new job to .github/workflows/ci.yml that runs govulncheck. Use 'go install golang.org/x/vuln/cmd/govulncheck@latest' then 'govulncheck ./...' (or use the golang/govulncheck-action if preferred). Run on ubuntu-latest with go-version: stable. This should be its own job parallel to the existing lint, build-and-test, cross-compile, smoke-tests, and integration-tests jobs.

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in CI Govulncheck is complete and correct

breadcrumbs.text: Added govulncheck job to .github/workflows/ci.yml: installs govulncheck@latest and runs against ./..., parallel to other CI jobs

Audit reviewed 1 file (.github/workflows/ci.yml), 14 lines added in commit dfcefb4. PASS. One accepted finding: govulncheck installed at @latest rather than a pinned version. This is standard for vulnerability scanners where database freshness matters more than reproducibility. The job follows the exact structure of all other CI jobs (ubuntu-latest, checkout@v6, setup-go@v6 stable). No correctness, security, or quality issues. Clean, minimal work.

audit.result_summary: Added a govulncheck CI job to .github/workflows/ci.yml that installs govulncheck@latest and runs it against the full module (./...). The job runs on ubuntu-latest with Go stable, parallel to all existing CI jobs. Audit confirmed correctness: YAML structure matches established patterns, no issues found. The @latest pin is intentional for vulnerability scanning freshness.

audit.objective: Verify the govulncheck CI job addition is complete, correct, and high-quality
audit.what_happened: Read the single modified file (.github/workflows/ci.yml, 14 lines added). Ran build, full test suite, and gofmt. All clean. Reviewed YAML structure against existing job patterns. Found one minor item (unpinned @latest) and accepted it.
went_well: The implementation is minimal and convention-following, making audit straightforward
improvements: None identified; the change is appropriately scoped
action_items: Monitor first CI run to confirm govulncheck passes on current dependency set (carried from task-0001 AAR)

task-0001.objective: Add a govulncheck job to the CI workflow, running parallel to existing jobs
task-0001.what_happened: Added a govulncheck job to .github/workflows/ci.yml between smoke-tests and integration-tests. The job checks out code, sets up Go stable, installs govulncheck@latest, and runs govulncheck ./... against the full module.
went_well: Straightforward addition following the existing job pattern; no structural changes needed
improvements: Could pin govulncheck to a specific version instead of @latest for reproducibility
action_items: Monitor first CI run to confirm govulncheck passes cleanly on the current dependency set
